parsed.rationale: No source node is directly achievable—the only source node any_log has no nearby logs/trees in state, so we should search for wood.
No source node is directly achievable: inventory and craftables are empty and no nearby logs are present, so we must search for any_log.
Direct collect: source node any_log is achievable now because spruce_log is nearby and no tool is required.
Direct: any_plank is a source node and is craftable now since inventory has spruce_log satisfying satisfied_inputs any_log×3, so crafting is immediately possible without a workstation.
Direct: stick is a source node and listed in craftable_items, and its satisfied_inputs use any_plank which we have as spruce_planks, so crafting it now is valid.
Direct craft: crafting_table is a source node and is craftable now (listed in craftable_items) using any_plank x4 as per satisfied_inputs.
Direct craft: wooden_pickaxe is a source node and is craftable now per craftable_items with sticks and any_plank using a crafting_table as indicated by satisfied_inputs and inventory.
Direct action because cobblestone is a source node, stone is nearby, and a wooden_pickaxe is available per state/satisfied_inputs, so collecting it now is supported.

Direct: stone_pickaxe is a source node and is in craftable_items; inventory has cobblestone×3 and sticks×2 with a crafting_table per satisfied_inputs, enabling the tool needed to mine raw_iron toward smelting.
Direct: the source-node furnace is craftable now since craftable_items includes furnace and inventory has 10 cobblestone with a crafting_table, matching the satisfied_inputs.
Search, because the only source node raw_iron isn’t directly achievable with no iron_ore nearby, even though the stone_pickaxe tool and furnace/fuel inputs are already available.
Direct: raw_iron is a source node and iron_ore is nearby while a stone_pickaxe (as per satisfied_inputs) is in inventory, so collecting it now is achievable.
